memory:memory/2026-04-07.md:65:84.snippet: - PoolGenius: structurally CANNOT do personalized coordination — their own admission. Recommending same picks to 50K+ subscribers collapses EV. SurvivorPulse's personalized model is an architecture-level moat. - Neither competitor ever shows: "Your 3 entries this week: Chiefs, Eagles, Bills — here's why each is different" - Neither shows a correlated elimination risk score for personal entries - PoolGenius requires purchase before seeing ANY tool UI — full cold paywall ### Prototype Redesign Direction (decided 2026-04-08) Stop: trying to prove survival math superiority through season replay Start: demonstrating the unified portfolio view that neither competitor shows Three demo moments to

memory:memory/2026-04-05.md:22:48.snippet: **Doctrine (frozen):** "SurvivorPulse helps serious survivor players manage correlation, future value, and risk across their entries and pools, because different teams can still be the same bet." **What it IS:** Decision-support system, portfolio-risk management tool, deterministic, risk-allocation focused **What it is NOT:** Pick list, favorites engine, content site, gambling tout **ICP (validated):** - Primary: Serious multi-entry player (3-10+ entries), manages pick distribution, uses tools like PoolCrunch/SurvivorGrid, currently allocates by gut/spreadsheet - Secondary: Scaling player (1-2 entries, looking to go to 3+) **Offseason Objectives (frozen):** 1. memory:memory/2026-04-11.md:98:122.snippet: ## Late Night: Backtesting Research Rounds 6-8 (Discord #backtest-research) ### Round 6: Entry-Count Scaling (168 runs) - 14 strategies x 4 entry counts (5, 10, 20, 50) x 3 seasons - 70/30 Blend loses crown at n=10 (beaten by 7 strategies), reclaims at n=50 - SP Production wins n=10 (+18 vs 70/30), Mixed Portfolio wins n=20 (+20) - CMEA thesis partially confirmed: coordination helps at n=10-20 but not n=50 (team exhaustion) - Coordinated Diversification (same scorer, smarter assignment) = 70/30 exactly.
